Class LatencyRecorder

java.lang.Object
io.perl.api.LatencyRecord
io.perl.api.LatencyRecorder
Direct Known Subclasses:
LatencyWindow
@NotThreadSafe public sealed class LatencyRecorder extends LatencyRecord permits LatencyWindow
Recorder that accumulates latency statistics and enforces configured bounds (min/max latencies and total thresholds). This class is intentionally NotThreadSafe and is expected to be used by a single recorder thread.

Units and behavior:

  • Latency values passed to record(long, long, long) are in the time unit used by the caller; callers should convert to the logger's time unit if necessary.
  • isOverflow() returns true when any of the configured total thresholds (latency sum, bytes, or records) is exceeded.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected final long
    highLatency
    Maximum accepted latency value (above this, samples are discarded).
    protected final long
    lowLatency
    Minimum accepted latency value (below this, samples are discarded).
    protected final long
    totalBytesMax
    Maximum allowed total bytes before overflow.
    protected final long
    totalLatencyMax
    Maximum allowed cumulative latency before overflow.
    protected final long
    totalRecordsMax
    Maximum allowed total records before overflow.

  • Field Details

    • lowLatency

      protected final long lowLatency
      Minimum accepted latency value (below this, samples are discarded).

    • highLatency

      protected final long highLatency
      Maximum accepted latency value (above this, samples are discarded).

    • totalLatencyMax

      protected final long totalLatencyMax
      Maximum allowed cumulative latency before overflow.

    • totalRecordsMax

      protected final long totalRecordsMax
      Maximum allowed total records before overflow.

    • totalBytesMax

      protected final long totalBytesMax
      Maximum allowed total bytes before overflow.

  • Constructor Details

    • LatencyRecorder

      public LatencyRecorder(long baseLatency, long latencyThreshold, long totalLatencyMax, long totalRecordsMax, long totalBytesMax)
      Construct a LatencyRecorder with configured thresholds.
      Parameters:
      baseLatency - minimum valid latency
      latencyThreshold - maximum valid latency
      totalLatencyMax - maximum cumulative latency
      totalRecordsMax - maximum total records
      totalBytesMax - maximum total bytes

  • Method Details

    • isOverflow

      public final boolean isOverflow()
      Checks whether any configured thresholds have been exceeded.
      Returns:
      true if overflow conditions occurred

    • update

      public final void update(long totalRecords, long totalLatency, long totalBytes, long invalidLatencyRecords, long lowerLatencyDiscardRecords, long higherLatencyDiscardRecords, long validLatencyRecords, long minLatency, long maxLatency)
      Update the recorder counters by adding the provided values.
      Parameters:
      totalRecords - total number of records to add
      totalLatency - total latency to add
      totalBytes - total bytes to add
      invalidLatencyRecords - number of invalid latency records to add
      lowerLatencyDiscardRecords - number of lower-discarded records to add
      higherLatencyDiscardRecords - number of higher-discarded records to add
      validLatencyRecords - number of valid latency records to add
      minLatency - minimum latency observed (used to update min)
      maxLatency - maximum latency observed (used to update max)

    • update

      public final void update(LatencyRecord record)
      Update using another LatencyRecord instance.
      Parameters:
      record - LatencyRecord instance whose counters will be merged into this recorder

    • record

      public final boolean record(long events, long bytes, long latency)
      Record latency for a batch of events. Returns true if the latency was considered valid (within low/high thresholds) and counted as valid.
      Parameters:
      events - number of events(records).
      bytes - number of bytes.
      latency - latency value in milliseconds (or caller-defined unit).
      Returns:
      true if this record is valid and counted; false otherwise.
